Los Angeles editing work

Video Editor Jobs in Los Angeles

Los Angeles editing roles can be remote, hybrid, or studio-side. The local market often rewards editors who can support shoots, agencies, creators, entertainment teams, and post workflows.

City pages give search engines specific local entry points without hiding that the first board is still launching.

Role examples

Early categories before live listings

Remote

Short-form video editor

Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, creator teams

  • fast turnaround
  • caption styling
  • social hooks
Remote or hybrid

YouTube channel editor

Long-form YouTube, podcasts, education, creator-led brands

  • story pacing
  • retention edits
  • thumbnail collaboration
Contract

Brand video editor

B2B, ecommerce, product launches, paid social

  • motion basics
  • ad variants
  • stakeholder review
Entry level

Assistant editor

Post houses, agencies, production teams

  • media management
  • proxy workflows
  • Premiere or Resolve

Local role patterns

Los Angeles roles often cluster around entertainment, creator channels, agency work, trailers, social content, branded video, podcasts, and assistant editor workflows.

What local employers should include

A useful Los Angeles listing should say whether the editor must be on-site, how media is shared, expected rate, union or non-union context where relevant, and whether production support is included.

Hiring teams

Post a video editor role early

Good listings should include content format, edit volume, software stack, compensation, timezone or location, revision cadence, and portfolio examples.

Connect this form to TalentPrism when the backend posting flow is ready.

Answers

Questions editors and hiring teams ask first

Are Los Angeles video editor jobs remote?

Some are remote, but many LA roles use hybrid workflows because footage, shoots, producers, and post teams may be local.
